# dynamodb-toolkit-fetch — extended reference

> Fetch adapter for `dynamodb-toolkit` v3. Serves the toolkit's standard REST route pack as a `(request: Request) => Promise<Response>` handler. Zero runtime dependencies; no framework peer dep.

## Scope

A thin translation layer between the Web Fetch handler shape and `dynamodb-toolkit`'s framework-agnostic pieces:

- `matchRoute(method, path)` from `dynamodb-toolkit/handler` — pure route-shape recognizer.
- Parsers from `dynamodb-toolkit/rest-core` — `parseFields`, `parseSort`, `parseFilter`, `parsePatch`, `parseNames`, `parsePaging`, `parseFlag`.
- Builders + policy from `dynamodb-toolkit/rest-core` — `buildEnvelope`, `buildErrorBody`, `paginationLinks`, `defaultPolicy`, `mapErrorStatus`, `mergePolicy`.
- A dynamodb-toolkit `Adapter` instance supplied by the caller.

## Public API

```ts
export interface FetchAdapterOptions<TItem extends Record<string, unknown>> {
  policy?: Partial<RestPolicy>;
  sortableIndices?: Record<string, string>;
  keyFromPath?: (rawKey: string, adapter: Adapter<TItem>) => Record<string, unknown>;
  exampleFromContext?: (query: Record<string, string>, body: unknown, request: Request) => Record<string, unknown>;
  maxBodyBytes?: number;
  mountPath?: string;
  onMiss?: (request: Request) => Response | null | Promise<Response | null> | undefined | void;
}

export function createFetchAdapter<TItem>(
  adapter: Adapter<TItem>,
  options?: FetchAdapterOptions<TItem>
): (request: Request) => Promise<Response>;

// With onMiss, return type widens:
export function createFetchAdapter<TItem>(
  adapter: Adapter<TItem>,
  options: FetchAdapterOptions<TItem> & {onMiss: NonNullable<FetchAdapterOptions<TItem>['onMiss']>}
): (request: Request) => Promise<Response | null>;
```

## Routes

Rooted at `options.mountPath` (or at `/` when no mount is configured):

- `GET /` — `getAll` (envelope with data / offset / limit / total / optional next+prev links)
- `POST /` — `post` (204 on success)
- `DELETE /` — `deleteAllByParams` (returns `{processed}`)
- `GET /-by-names`, `DELETE /-by-names` — `getByKeys` / `deleteByKeys`
- `PUT /-load` — `putAll` (body must be an array; 400 `BadLoadBody` otherwise)
- `PUT /-clone`, `PUT /-move` — `cloneAllByParams` / `moveAllByParams` (body is overlay)
- `PUT /-clone-by-names`, `PUT /-move-by-names` — `cloneByKeys` / `moveByKeys`
- `GET /:key` — `getByKey` (empty `policy.statusCodes.miss` on undefined)
- `PUT /:key` — `put` (body merged with URL key; `?force=true` toggles force)
- `PATCH /:key` — `patch` (meta-prefixed keys extracted as options via `parsePatch`)
- `DELETE /:key` — `delete`
- `PUT /:key/-clone`, `PUT /:key/-move` — `clone` / `move` (single-item, overlay body)

## Dispatch & error behavior

- Unknown route or off-mount path → `onMiss(request)` if provided, else empty `404 Response`.
- Known route shape with wrong method → `405 MethodNotAllowed`.
- Adapter throws → error runs through `policy.errorBody` + `mapErrorStatus` to produce a JSON body + status.
- Adapter throws with explicit `.status` in `[400, 599]` → status passes through verbatim.

## Body reading

`maxBodyBytes` (default 1 MiB) is enforced two ways on every body-reading route:

1. **Content-Length fast-path.** If the declared length exceeds the cap, reject `413 PayloadTooLarge` before reading any bytes.
2. **Mid-stream counter.** Otherwise read `request.body.getReader()` with a running byte total; reject `413` as soon as the counter crosses the cap. Handles chunked uploads and CL-liars.

Invalid JSON → `400 BadJsonBody`. Empty body → `null`.

## Composition — `onMiss`

Default (no `onMiss`): adapter is terminal. Unknown / off-mount requests resolve to an empty `404 Response`. Suitable for single-collection Workers and `Deno.serve(handler)`.

With `onMiss`: the hook decides. Return:
- `Response` → handler returns it.
- `null` → handler returns `null`, widening the caller's type so it can fall through to another handler.
- `undefined` → fall back to the default `404 Response`.

Example Hono composition:

```js
const planetsHandler = createFetchAdapter(planets, {mountPath: '/planets', onMiss: () => null});
app.all('/planets/*', async c => (await planetsHandler(c.req.raw)) ?? c.notFound());
```

## Target runtimes

- **Cloudflare Workers** — `export default {fetch: handler}`
- **Deno Deploy / Deno.serve** — `Deno.serve(handler)`
- **Bun.serve** — `Bun.serve({fetch: handler})`
- **Hono** — delegate from `c.req.raw`
- **itty-router** — catch-all route that defers to the handler
- **Node 20+** — any `(Request) => Promise<Response>` server

Every target runtime ships the Fetch globals. The adapter uses only `Request`, `Response`, `URL`, `URLSearchParams`, `TextDecoder`, and `ReadableStream`.

## Reference

- Parent toolkit: https://github.com/uhop/dynamodb-toolkit
- Sibling adapter (Koa): https://github.com/uhop/dynamodb-toolkit-koa
- Sibling adapter (Express): https://github.com/uhop/dynamodb-toolkit-express
- Route pack contract: https://github.com/uhop/dynamodb-toolkit/wiki/HTTP-handler
